skill.name: ownership-gate
skill.description: Verify that junior developers can explain and defend code they wrote before work is marked complete.
skill.summary: Run a structured ownership check before completing code mentoring tasks.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, weak-crypto keywords, and reconnaissance-like wording, but review found no executable code, shell commands, cryptography, network activity, or system inspection. The skill is a mentoring checklist with response templates and interview-preparation prompts, so the static findings are false positives.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Fences Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks are Markdown code-fence delimiters around response templates, not Ruby or shell execution. No command invocation or user-controlled execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is a Markdown response template. There is no executable script context, command name, interpolation, or shell invocation.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain natural-language coaching text about code understanding and failure modes. No hash, cipher, certificate, or cryptographic API appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is prose-only guidance and templates. The flagged lines do not contain cryptographic identifiers or implementation logic.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged lines ask a learner to explain design choices and record learning topics. They do not request hostnames, users, environment variables, files, processes, or other system inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantics are educational review prompts, not host reconnaissance. No command, API, or data collection target is specified.

content.value_statement: AI-assisted coding can let learners ship code they do not fully understand. This skill gives mentors a structured gate for checking explanation, trade-offs, edge cases, and readiness.
actual_capabilities: Provides a four-question ownership review sequence for code written by a learner.
Defines pass and blocked outcomes based on the learner's ability to explain their code.
Lists red flags that indicate copied code, weak reasoning, or missing edge-case awareness.
Includes response templates for blocked and passed ownership checks.
Guides mentors through Socratic recovery before blocking completion.
Connects code review results to future interview preparation notes.
limitations: Does not inspect code automatically or run static analysis.
Requires a mentor or reviewer to judge the learner's answers.
Does not replace security, testing, or production readiness reviews.
Works best when the reviewed code and requirements are available in context.

use_cases.title: Mentor a Junior Developer
use_cases.description: Use the gate after a learner finishes a task to confirm they can explain control flow, design choices, and edge cases.

use_cases.title: Review AI-Assisted Work
use_cases.description: Check whether a developer understands code generated with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code before accepting the work.

use_cases.title: Prepare for Technical Interviews
use_cases.description: Turn completed code into interview practice by asking the learner to explain trade-offs, failures, and patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Ownership Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review after a small coding task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ownership gate on my recent code change. Ask the questions one at a time and decide whether I pass or need review.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Design Reasoning
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a solution that has multiple possible approaches
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the ownership gate with extra focus on why this approach was chosen. Ask me about alternatives and trade-offs before deciding the result.

prompt_templates.title: Test Edge-Case Understanding
prompt_templates.scenario: Review code that handles inputs, errors, or asynchronous behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ownership gate and include edge-case questions specific to this code. Block completion if I cannot explain the failure modes.

prompt_templates.title: Create Interview Notes from the Gate
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced mentoring session after a completed feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the ownership gate, guide recovery if I struggle, then summarize the concepts I explained well and the topics I should practice for interviews.

output_examples.input: A learner can explain the main flow but cannot explain why the approach was chosen.
output_examples.output: The gate would pause completion, focus review on alternatives and trade-offs, then retry the reasoning question before passing.

output_examples.input: A learner explains the flow, names dependencies, and predicts an edge-case failure.
output_examples.output: The gate would pass and record the explained concept as a future interview talking point.

output_examples.input: A learner says the AI suggested the code and cannot trace sample data through it.
output_examples.output: The gate would block completion and direct the learner to review the specific sections until they can explain them.

best_practices: Ask questions in sequence and give the learner time to reason aloud.
Use code-specific edge cases instead of generic questions.
Guide recovery before blocking, then document what the learner still needs to understand.
anti_patterns: Passing a learner only because the code works.
Turning the gate into a lecture instead of asking the learner to explain.
Using the gate as a replacement for tests, security review, or production approval.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides a reviewer through a structured ownership check for code written by a junior developer.

faq.question: Can it automatically verify code quality?
faq.answer: No. It provides review prompts and decision guidance, but a human reviewer still judges the answers.

faq.question: Why can the gate block completion?
faq.answer: The skill treats understanding as required. If the learner cannot explain the code after guided review, the task should not be marked complete.

faq.question: Is this only for AI-generated code?
faq.answer: No. It works for any learner code, but it is especially useful when AI tools helped produce the solution.

faq.question: Does it include security review?
faq.answer: No. It may reveal misunderstandings, but it does not scan code or replace a security audit.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
